text.title: Diff-Coverage Examples
text.summary: Worked examples — one healthy rollout and one antipattern caught at PR review.

example.title: Python repo wiring diff-cover with billing override
example.note: Repo uses coverage.py and pytest. coverage-diff.yml sets default_threshold=80 with override billing/** at 90 and scripts/** at 0 (reason: ops scripts, not gated). PR adds a new field to the signup form: src/signup.py diff coverage 92%, billing/invoice_render.py untouched, scripts/migrate.py 0% (exempt). PR-comment headline reads "Diff coverage: 92% (gate pass)"; full-repo trend appears as a one-liner. Dashboard updates median_diff_coverage_7d to 84.2%. Merge passes.

example.title: Aggregate masking a failing billing file
example.note: Same repo, week later. PR diff covers src/landing.py at 95% and billing/charge.py at 35%. Aggregate is ~78% which under a flat 80% gate would fail — but a careless tool reports per-PR aggregate only. Without per-file enforcement, billing slips through. Validator (per-file rule + override at 90 for billing) rejects: f5 aggregate-masks-failing-file. Engineer adds two billing tests; second pass: billing/charge.py at 95%, merge passes.
